title: hedera helix var. hibernica
intro: Hedera helix var. hibernica, also known as the Irish ivy, is a variety of the English ivy plant native to Ireland and the UK. It is a climbing evergreen plant with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine. Hedera helix var. hibernica is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or mixing them with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
